LOOK.text: 'Patient Zero Rehabilitation Clinic'. Where they send wallets that went too deep into memecoin season.

USE.text: The fine print mentions 'No refunds, no regrets, only recovery'.

TALK.text: The sign doesn't talk. But it judges silently.

PICK.text: I can't take the sign, but I screenshot it for my portfolio losses.

OPEN.text: The sign doesn't open, but it opens my eyes to crypto addiction.

CLOSE.text: I can't close the sign, but I can close my trading app.

PUSH.text: I push the sign. It wobbles but holds. Built to last, unlike most DeFi protocols.

PULL.text: I pull the sign. The post is deep in the ground. Someone meant for this to stay.

GIVE.text: The sign doesn't accept patients anymore. Or gifts.

LOOK.text: A long winding path leads to the clinic. Hope I've got stamina... and a decent gas allowance.

else.text: The mountain path forks. Without directions, I could get lost up here for days.

TALK.text: The path echoes my footsteps back. Sounds like liquidation alerts.

PICK.text: I can't take the path, but I can follow it to recovery... or more losses.

OPEN.text: The path is always open. A one-way road to financial therapy.

CLOSE.text: I can't close the path, but I can close my positions.

PUSH.text: I push forward on the path. Each step feels heavier. Like gas fees on a congested chain.

PULL.text: I pull back from the path. Maybe I'm not ready for recovery yet.

GIVE.text: The path doesn't want anything. It just wants me to walk it.

LOOK.text: Clouds shift like unstable protocols. Reality here is barely forked.

USE.text: The clouds form patterns that look like candlestick charts.

TALK.text: The wind whispers: 'Not your keys, not your clouds...'

PICK.text: I try to grab a cloud. It turns into vaporware.

OPEN.text: The sky is always open. The ultimate open source.

CLOSE.text: The sky can't be closed. It's censorship-resistant.

PUSH.text: I push against the sky. Surprisingly, nothing happens.

PULL.text: I try to pull the clouds closer. They're on a different protocol.

GIVE.text: I throw my hopes at the sky. They come back down.

LOOK.text: Something's moving in the shadows... could be a dev debugging or just my paranoia.

USE.text: I use the trees for cover. Maybe the clinic staff won't see me coming.

TALK.text: The trees whisper forgotten secrets about patients who never returned.

PICK.text: I can't take the trees. They're rooted. Unlike most crypto projects.

OPEN.text: The trees provide a natural barrier. No key required.

CLOSE.text: I close my eyes under the trees. Pretend this is all a bad dream.

PUSH.text: I push through the tree line. Branches scratch my face. Worth it? No.

PULL.text: I pull a branch. It snaps back. The forest has a no-return policy.

GIVE.text: The trees accept only CO2. I'm not qualified.

LOOK.text: Crystal clear water trickling from the rocks. Decentralized hydration.

USE.text: I splash some water on my face. Refreshing. Nature's cooldown function.

TALK.text: The water babbles. It's the original proof-of-flow.

else.text: I fill an empty bottle with spring water. H2O — the original proof-of-work.

OPEN.text: I can't open a spring. It's already open-source.

CLOSE.text: I can't close a mountain spring. That's censorship.

PUSH.text: I push against the rock. The water keeps flowing. Nature doesn't pause.

PULL.text: I can't pull water uphill. Gravity is Layer 0.

GIVE.text: The spring gives ME water. Not the other way around.

LOOK.text: A cabin perched on the hillside, warm light glowing from inside. Smoke curls from the chimney. Someone is home. Or something.

USE.text: Too far away. I need to follow the winding path up there.

TALK.text: I shout toward the cabin. The mountains echo back: '...NGMI...NGMI...NGMI...'

PICK.text: I can't pick up a building. That's not how real estate works, even on-chain.

OPEN.text: I need to get up there first. The path is the only way.

CLOSE.text: I can't close a building from down here. I can barely close browser tabs.

PUSH.text: I push toward the clinic in my mind. My body stays put. Visualization doesn't work on buildings.

PULL.text: I try to will the cabin closer. Gravity doesn't support pull requests.

GIVE.text: I'd give anything to be up there already. But the mountain demands the journey.

LOOK.text: Signs along the path: 'TURN BACK — PORTFOLIO AHEAD'. 'LAST CHANCE TO TAKE PROFIT'. 'NO FINANCIAL ADVICE BEYOND THIS POINT'.

USE.text: The signs are bolted down. Like a smart contract — immutable.

TALK.text: The wind whistles through the signs. It sounds like 'HODL'.

PICK.text: These signs are cemented into the ground. Not going anywhere.

OPEN.text: There's nothing to open on these signs.

CLOSE.text: The path is one-way. You can't close a warning.

LOOK.text: Massive boulders covered in lichen. They've been here since before Bitcoin. Since before money, even. The OG store of value.

USE.text: I lean against a boulder. It's warm from the sunset. Like a hardware wallet that's been mining too long.

TALK.text: I address the boulder: 'You've been HODLing this spot for millennia. Respect.' The boulder says nothing. Diamond hands.

PICK.text: These boulders weigh several tons each. My inventory has a strict carry limit. And a gas limit.

OPEN.text: I check for cracks. One boulder has a fissure with quartz crystals inside. Nature's own proof-of-work.

CLOSE.text: I can't close a boulder. They're permanently open-source. Geologically speaking.

PUSH.text: I push the boulder. It doesn't move. Sisyphus had the same problem, but at least his boulder didn't have a market cap.

PULL.text: I try to pull a small rock free. It tumbles down the slope. Hope nobody was down there. Not my liability.

GIVE.text: The boulders accept nothing. They are the original cold storage.

LOOK.text: Jagged purple peaks piercing the sunset sky. Each mountain looks like a different candlestick pattern. That one's a doji. That one's a shooting star.

USE.text: I can't use mountains. They use ME. For scale.

TALK.text: I yodel into the void. The mountains echo back something that sounds suspiciously like 'BUY THE DIP.'

PICK.text: I try to pick a mountain. Even God needed six days to make these. I have maybe six minutes.

OPEN.text: If I could open a mountain, I'd probably find a dragon sitting on a pile of ETH.

CLOSE.text: Mountains don't close. They're the original 24/7 market.

PUSH.text: I push toward the mountains. They get no closer. Like reaching profitability.

PULL.text: I can't pull mountains. Tectonic plates already tried. Takes millions of years. No gas optimization for that.

GIVE.text: The mountains demand nothing. They're the only thing in crypto that doesn't ask for your seed phrase.

LOOK.text: Wild alpine flowers blooming in golden and white. They thrive without governance, without tokenomics, without a roadmap. True decentralization.

USE.text: I smell the flowers. For a moment, I forget about gas fees and impermanent loss. Then the moment passes.

TALK.text: I whisper to the flowers: 'How do you grow without venture capital?' They don't answer. Trade secret.

PICK.text: I pick a wildflower. It wilts immediately. Off-chain flowers don't survive inventory transfer.

OPEN.text: I open a flower bud. A tiny bee flies out, annoyed. Decentralized pollination disrupted.

CLOSE.text: Flowers close at night. It's their version of market hours. I should respect that.

PUSH.text: I push through the flowers. They bounce right back. More resilient than most L2s.

PULL.text: I pull a flower. Its roots hold firm. It's been staking in this soil for years.

GIVE.text: The flowers give ME oxygen. Best yield I've seen all year.

lines: The printout shows coordinates. A clinic in the mountains.
Dr. Satoshi runs the operation. Treats on-chain addiction.
Patient Zero escaped. And you're getting closer to finding out why.
The printout shows coordinates. A clinic in the mountains.
Dr. Satoshi runs the operation. Treats on-chain addiction.
Patient Zero escaped. And you're getting closer to finding out why.
